title: Purpose and roles of HRM
summary: 9609 AS — HRM purpose, key functions, strategic role, and link to business objectives.
learningObjectives: Define HRM and explain its strategic purpose.
Outline key HRM functions across the employment cycle.
Distinguish strategic HRM from traditional personnel management.
Explain how HRM supports business objectives.

simpleExplanation.title: Managing people strategically
simpleExplanation.summary: Human resource management ensures the business has the right number of people, with the right skills, motivation, and cost, to achieve its objectives.
steps: Workforce planning — forecast future staffing needs.
Recruitment and selection — attract and choose candidates.
Training and development — build skills and careers.
Performance management, motivation, and employee relations.

flashcards.back: Acquire, develop, and retain workforce to meet business objectives efficiently and legally.
HRM is strategic and integrated with business goals; personnel was often administrative/payroll focused.
Treats employees as valuable assets — investment in training, participation, empowerment.
Employees as cost to control — flexible contracts, minimal training, performance targets.
HRM designs reward and job design to motivate (Topic 2.2).
HR supplies skilled labour for production and customer service (4.1).
Ensure compliance with employment law — contracts, discrimination, H&S (6.1.1).

sections.content: **Purpose and roles of HRM** (2.1.1) opens **Topic 2 People**. Cambridge tests **application** — how HR policy helps the **case firm** achieve objectives (growth, quality, cost control), not textbook definitions alone.

items: **Workforce planning (2.1.2)** — match future labour supply to demand.
**Recruitment & selection (2.1.3)** — fill vacancies with suitable staff.
**Training & development (2.1.6)** — upskill for current and future roles.
**Morale, welfare & relations (2.1.5, 2.1.7)** — retain staff, manage conflict.

sections.question: A fast-growing online retailer struggles with delivery delays and customer complaints. Explain two HRM roles that could help.
sections.solution:
**1. Recruitment & selection:** Hire more **delivery drivers and warehouse staff** with reliable track record; reduce bottlenecks affecting **operations** (4.1).

**2. Training & development:** **Induction and customer service training** for new hires; **on-the-job coaching** to improve picking accuracy — links to **quality** (9.2) and brand reputation.

Also consider **workforce planning** to forecast peaks (e.g. holidays) and **motivation** (2.2) to reduce turnover of trained staff.
